reviewer_context: fresh subagent invocation, no authoring session memory
review_invocation: general-purpose subagent with adversarial-review prompt; dogfood of the AC-2 plan-time-gate rule on day 1 (gate not yet implemented; this is a manual bootstrap)

single_concern_assessment.rationale: The noun 'enforced engineering rigour' is broad enough to absorb almost anything. AC-1 alone bundles six mechanisms (verify-slice.sh + 3 hooks + ESLint config + checksums + coverage gate); AC-4 bundles five distinct subagent gates. Per CLAUDE.md 'Small, single-purpose functions' and the slice-sizing 'if you reach for AND, split' rule the slice itself is meant to enforce, this should be at least: (a) safety-net foundation, (b) plan-time gate, (c) pair-review hook, (d) subagent-suite (split further), (e) multi-provider reviewer + structured findings, (f) ratchet + audit + CLAUDE.md rewrite. The slice violates the rule it's bootstrapping, which is a credibility problem for the rule.

scope_completeness_findings.description: Cited failure mode 'definition-of-done drift' has no dedicated AC. AC-9 rewrites CLAUDE.md but no mechanism programmatically enforces DoD-completeness on a slice.
scope_completeness_findings.recommendation: Add AC requiring a DoD-evidence-parser (each item in spec 72 §11 + 6-item DoD must have either evidence link or explicit deferral string in verification.md, parsed and PR-blocking).

scope_completeness_findings.description: Cited 'optimistic deferrals compounding' is partially addressed (allowlist expiry in AC-7) but not for skill-finding deferrals. A reviewer can mark every finding 'disputed: noted' and ship.
scope_completeness_findings.recommendation: AC-3/6 should require deferral reasons themselves to be reviewed (subagent or schema constraint: deferral strings must reference a tracked issue ID or a future slice ID).

scope_completeness_findings.description: Spec 72 §11 security checklist (13 items) is not threaded into AC. AC-6 mentions /security-review schema but says nothing about parsing the 13-item checklist into structured evidence per slice.
scope_completeness_findings.recommendation: Bind AC-6's parser to spec 72 §11 explicitly: each box becomes a required JSON field in security.md schema.

bootstrapping_findings.description: AC-1 'meta-test fixtures for verify-slice.sh (test-first per the rule we're enforcing)' is recursively defined: no PRIOR enforcement exists to verify the meta-tests themselves were written first. Honour-system commit for the very rule we're abandoning honour-system for.
bootstrapping_findings.recommendation: Either accept this as a bounded honour-system bootstrap (document explicitly + have the multi-provider reviewer audit commit ordering on the bootstrap slice), or make the bootstrap manual-verified: a HANDOFF section showing two commits with separated SHAs (failing test commit, then implementation commit).

bootstrapping_findings.description: Session 36 ships verify-slice.sh skeleton + pre-commit-verify hook registered. From session 37 onward every commit must satisfy verify-slice.sh — but session 36 itself is exempt. Same 'rules don't apply to me' pattern the slice exists to fix.
bootstrapping_findings.recommendation: Either land verify-slice.sh in two commits (skeleton, then immediate self-pass commit) within session 36, or scope session 36 strictly to docs + tests-that-fail, with session 37 doing both impl AND first dogfood pass in one breath.

bootstrapping_findings.description: Session 42 bundles AC-8 + AC-9 + slice merge in 250 lines. AC-9 alone (CLAUDE.md 'Hard controls' rewrite cataloguing every gate + bypass procedure) plausibly consumes 250 lines.
bootstrapping_findings.recommendation: Split AC-8 to session 41 or expand session-42 budget.

budget_assessment.rationale: AC-5 multi-provider PR reviewer alone ~300-500 lines. AC-7 Stryker config + ratchet + slice-bound allowlist ~400 lines. AC-3 pair-programming hook + intent file + finding-response loop + persistent reviewer memory ~400. AC-4's five subagent gates each ~150 = 750. AC-9 'Hard controls' rewrite ≥ 200. Estimate appears to be vibes, not bottom-up; cite the line-count assumption per AC.

scope_boundary_findings.description: Out-of-scope 'Branch protection rule changes on main' but DoD item 9 requires it for slice completion. DoD that depends on a non-tracked external action with no verifier is unfalsifiable.
scope_boundary_findings.recommendation: Either (a) add an in-scope verifier (gh API call in scripts/audit-controls.sh that asserts main has required PR reviews enabled), or (b) move DoD-9 to a separate ops checklist outside slice DoD.

scope_boundary_findings.description: In-scope 'CLAUDE.md updates (final session only)' contradicts 'Surgical changes — touch only what the task requires'. Deferring all CLAUDE.md edits to the last session means session-36's controls cannot reference CLAUDE.md sections that don't exist yet.
scope_boundary_findings.recommendation: Permit incremental CLAUDE.md additions per session for the 'Hard controls' table; AC-9's final session is the rewrite/consolidation pass, not the only edit.

scope_boundary_findings.description: 'Blocks: All feature work — including S-F7-β cleanup' — 7 sessions of infrastructure with β cleanup parked. Gold-plating before delivery antipattern; β presumably has user-facing value being held hostage to speculative tooling that may be over-engineered.
scope_boundary_findings.recommendation: Make AC-1 + AC-2 mandatory before β resumes (the foundational + plan-gate layers); ACs 3-9 can run in parallel with β cleanup using AC-1's safety net.

missing_concerns.concern: Spec-quote enforcement is in AC-4b but only triggers on text matching 'per spec X'. The failure mode is broader: paraphrasing a spec without invoking 'per spec'.
missing_concerns.rationale: Stronger gate: any HANDOFF or verification.md mention of a spec number triggers a quote-presence check.

missing_concerns.concern: No control prevents the author from disabling a hook by editing settings.json. Checksums protect hook bodies; nothing protects the registration entry.
missing_concerns.rationale: Add settings.json hook-registration checksum to AC-1's hooks-checksums.txt scope.

missing_concerns.concern: No mechanism enforces the 'AC arithmetic check' rule from CLAUDE.md (Σ in-scope rows = total rows). Sigma check in this very acceptance.md is hand-waved.
missing_concerns.rationale: AC-2 plan-time gate should run an automated AC-arithmetic verifier on acceptance.md.

missing_concerns.concern: No 'snapshot before refactor' enforcement (CLAUDE.md engineering convention).
missing_concerns.rationale: Pre-edit hook could detect refactor signature (>50 lines or >2 files in one Edit batch) and require explicit checkpoint commit reference.

missing_concerns.concern: No control on the 'Verify before planning' rule. Author claims branch tips ('a3f67ec', '92f77d7') in acceptance.md without showing verification.
missing_concerns.rationale: Plan-gate subagent (AC-2) should verify any SHA / branch / state assertion in acceptance.md against actual git.

self_modification_risk_findings.description: hooks-checksums.txt is itself a regular file in the repo. Author can update both a hook AND the checksum in the same commit — integrity check passes, control is silently weakened. The session-integrity hook can be bypassed by editing it first then regenerating its own checksum.
self_modification_risk_findings.recommendation: (a) Sign checksums file with a key the user controls (not committed in repo); or (b) require any change to .claude/hooks/** OR hooks-checksums.txt to carry a 'control-change' label that triggers the multi-provider reviewer (AC-5) plus a tightening-only ratchet (AC-7); or (c) append-only audit log in a separate location verifying checksum changes are accompanied by reasoning.

self_modification_risk_findings.description: Bypass procedure 'requires multi-key sign-off' is mentioned in AC-9 but undefined. With one human + one Claude account, 'multi-key' is theatrical.
self_modification_risk_findings.recommendation: Specify the bypass quorum concretely (e.g. user GPG-signed commit + multi-provider reviewer green) or honestly call it 'user-only override with audit log'.

self_modification_risk_findings.description: Author can lower coverage threshold or shrink mutation-testing scope in the same commit that 'tightens' something else. AC-7 ratchet says thresholds can only go UP, but the ratchet script is itself in-repo and modifiable.
self_modification_risk_findings.recommendation: Ratchet check should compare against origin/main HEAD threshold values, not a configurable file; require a separate signed-commit override.

dod_quality_findings.description: DoD-5 'Coverage on this slice's own scripts ≥ 90%' — coverage of bash hooks is poorly defined; vitest doesn't cover .sh files. Trivially satisfiable if the metric measures only .ts surfaces.
dod_quality_findings.recommendation: Specify per-language coverage: bats / shellspec for .sh hooks at ≥80% line, vitest at ≥90% line for .ts utilities.

dod_quality_findings.description: DoD-6 'Adversarial review skills run + outputs structured + addressed' is weakly testable. 'Addressed' has no schema definition at this level; could be self-marked.
dod_quality_findings.recommendation: Bind to AC-6 schema: every finding has resolution status + (if 'disputed') a multi-provider-reviewer-acknowledged reason.

dod_quality_findings.description: DoD-2 'verify-slice.sh itself passes against this slice (it dogfoods)' — but the slice writes verify-slice.sh. Tautology unless verify-slice.sh tests are independently authored / reviewed.
dod_quality_findings.recommendation: DoD-2 should require: verify-slice.sh passes against (a) this slice, AND (b) one prior reference slice (e.g. S-F7-α) without modification. Catches the 'wrote tests to match my output' failure mode.

dod_quality_findings.description: DoD has 10 items but DoD-9 is an external user action (not slice-author verifiable) and DoD-7 depends on AC-5 which depends on user provisioning an API key. Slice can be 'done' modulo two external blockers indefinitely.
dod_quality_findings.recommendation: Mark DoD-7 + DoD-9 as 'external preconditions'; track them in a separate prerequisites table; gate slice merge on them being satisfied with timestamps.

dod_quality_findings.description: DoD does not include the spec 72 §11 security checklist (13 items) which CLAUDE.md 'Engineering conventions' lists as mandatory per slice ('Plus the 13-item security checklist in spec 72 §11. No exceptions.').
dod_quality_findings.recommendation: Add DoD-11: 'spec 72 §11 13-item security checklist evidenced in security.md or explicitly deferred per spec 72 §11 exemption pattern.'

summary: The slice has a strong intent and a real failure-mode catalogue, but it violates the slice-sizing rule it is bootstrapping, materially under-budgets the implementation, leaves the security-checklist tie-in implicit, and has bypassable self-modification protections. Recommend splitting into 2-3 sub-slices (foundation; subagent-suite; multi-provider+ratchet+rewrite), unblocking β after the foundation lands rather than after the whole programme, and hardening the checksum/ratchet integrity story before any code lands.
